requirements.desc: When logs.prompts is true, callAgent/callAgentAsync shall append one JSON Lines entry per invocation
requirements.note: appendAgentLog checks cfg?.logs?.prompts and calls fs.appendFileSync with JSON.stringify(agentLog.toJSON())+"\n"; both callAgent (finally) and callAgentAsync (then/catch) invoke it

requirements.desc: When logs.prompts is false or absent, no log processing shall occur
requirements.note: appendAgentLog returns early with `if (!cfg?.logs?.prompts) return;` covering both false and absent cases

requirements.desc: When AgentLog is not passed, no log processing shall occur
requirements.note: Both callAgent and callAgentAsync guard all agentLog operations with `if (agentLog)` null checks; appendAgentLog is only called inside those guards

requirements.desc: Each log entry shall contain executeStartAt, executeEndAt, executeTime, spec, phase, prompt
requirements.note: AgentLog.toJSON() returns all six fields; all are assigned before appendAgentLog is called in both sync and async paths

requirements.desc: When callAgent/callAgentAsync fails, the log entry shall still be appended
requirements.note: callAgent uses try/finally so appendAgentLog runs on throw; callAgentAsync uses .then(ok,err) finish() pattern covering rejection

requirements.desc: logs.dir sets log file path; defaults to agent.workDir/logs/prompts.jsonl
requirements.note: resolveLogDir returns cfg.logs.dir when set, otherwise path.join(resolve(cwd, agent.workDir||'.tmp'), 'logs'); file is always prompts.jsonl

requirements.desc: logs/ directory shall be created automatically
requirements.note: appendAgentLog calls fs.mkdirSync(logDir, { recursive: true }) before writing

requirements.desc: No size limit or rotation in v1
requirements.note: Uses fs.appendFileSync with no size check or rotation logic; draft and spec both document this as intentional

requirements.desc: Log write failure shall not be re-thrown; output to stderr; no retry
requirements.note: appendAgentLog wraps mkdirSync+appendFileSync in try/catch, writes to process.stderr, returns without rethrowing; no retry logic present

unplanned.change: Added LogsConfig typedef and validateConfig validation block for logs.prompts and logs.dir — not in requirements but directly supports the feature's config schema
Imports 'before' and 'after' from node:test but neither is used anywhere in the file — dead imports identified in review.md item 4 (verdict REJECTED, so they remain)
Dual-timestamp pattern: agentLog.executeStartAt set via new Date() and executeTime computed from separate startMs = Date.now(), causing minor drift; review item 2 was APPROVED but not applied
callAgentAsync uses manual then(ok,err) finish() pattern instead of Promise.prototype.finally; review item 3 was APPROVED but not applied
AgentLog mutation logic (prompt, executeStartAt assignment) remains scattered in agent.js rather than extracted to AgentLog.start()/end() methods; review item 1 was APPROVED but not applied

summary.notes: All 9 requirements are fully satisfied. The implementation correctly handles all P1/P2/P3 requirements. Three code-quality improvements from the review (start/end methods, dual-timestamp elimination, Promise.finally) were approved but not applied — these are style/quality gaps, not requirement gaps. Two minor residual issues: unused 'before'/'after' imports in the test file, and the dual-timestamp inconsistency in timing fields.
